commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: fix(config): accept model thinkingLevelMap in provider config schema (#91011) [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary
The strict Zod schema that validates persisted provider model entries (`models.providers.*.models[]`) did not allow a `thinkingLevelMap` field, even though it is a first-class part of the model definition (present on the runtime type and the read-path TypeBox schema). As a result, any config write that includes a model `thinkingLevelMap` failed `.strict()` validation and the atomic write rolled back.

This bites the built-in **Microsoft Foundry Entra ID** onboarding: its auth writer emits `thinkingLevelMap` on each model entry, so onboarding passes the connection test and then can't persist ŌĆö leaving the provider unconfigured with no supported way to finish (#91011).

## Root cause
- `thinkingLevelMap` is part of the model definition: runtime type `ThinkingLevelMap` (`packages/llm-core/src/types.ts`) and the read-path schema `ThinkingLevelMapSchema` (`src/agents/sessions/model-registry.ts`).
- But the **write-path** Zod schema `ModelDefinitionSchema` (`src/config/zod-schema.core.ts`), which validates `models.providers.*.models[]` on save, is `.strict()` and was missing the key ŌåÆ unrecognized key ŌåÆ the atomic config write rolls back. (`normalizeResolvedModel` re-adds the field in memory at request time, so it never surfaces on the read path ŌĆö only on write.)

## Changes
- `src/config/zod-schema.core.ts` ŌĆö add `ModelThinkingLevelMapSchema` (mirrors the canonical 7 `ModelThinkingLevel` keys exactly: `off/minimal/low/medium/high/xhigh/max`, values `string | null`, strict) and wire it into `ModelDefinitionSchema`. Add bidirectional `AssertAssignable` guards tying `z.infer<ŌĆ”>` to the canonical `ThinkingLevelMap` type so the two can't drift.
- `src/config/zod-schema.models.test.ts` ŌĆö schema accepts a provider model with `thinkingLevelMap`.
- `extensions/microsoft-foundry/index.test.ts` ŌĆö Foundry auth writer emits the `thinkingLevelMap` shape on its model entry.

One logical change; additive only (no deletions); no new dependency.

## Verification (local, Node 22)
```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/zod-schema.models.test.ts        # 2/2 pass
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/microsoft-foundry/index.test.ts  # 41/41 pass
pnpm tsgo:core                                                          # exit 0 (type guards compile)
pnpm check:base-config-schema                                           # ok
```

## Real behavior proof
Reproduced end-to-end through the real `openclaw config validate` command (no Azure/Entra account required ŌĆö the bug is the config write/validate path).

**Behavior addressed:** persisted provider model config containing `thinkingLevelMap` was rejected by strict validation, rolling back the write (blocks Foundry Entra onboarding).

**Real environment tested:** local OpenClaw CLI built from source, macOS arm64, Node v22.22.3, run against an isolated config via `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`.

**Setup ŌĆö `/tmp/openclaw-foundry-repro.json`** (mirrors what the Foundry Entra writer emits):
```json
{
  "models": {
    "providers": {
      "microsoft-foundry": {
        "baseUrl": "https://example.services.ai.azure.com/openai/v1",
        "api": "openai-completions",
        "models": [
          {
            "id": "gpt-5.4",
            "name": "gpt-5.4",
            "reasoning": true,
            "thinkingLevelMap": { "off": "none", "minimal": null, "low": "low", "medium": "medium", "high": "high" }
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}
```

**Exact command run after this patch:**
```
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-foundry-repro.json pnpm openclaw config validate
```

**Evidence ŌĆö before (on `main`, unfixed):**
```
OpenClaw config is invalid: /tmp/openclaw-foundry-repro.json
  ├Ś models.providers.microsoft-foundry.models.0: Invalid input
Run `openclaw doctor --fix` to repair, or fix the keys above manually.
# exit code 1
```
(The strict `ModelDefinitionSchema` rejects the `thinkingLevelMap` key at that exact path ŌĆö the same rejection the issue reporter saw as `Unrecognized key: "thinkingLevelMap"`.)

**Evidence / observed result ŌĆö after (this branch):**
```
Config valid: /tmp/openclaw-foundry-repro.json
# exit code 0
```

**What was not tested:** the live Azure Foundry Entra managed-identity onboarding flow (no Entra resource available); validated through the config write/validate path that the onboarding writer feeds, which is where the rollback occurred.

## AI-assisted
This change was AI-assisted. It was implemented by one model and independently reviewed by a different model (code read end-to-end; findings applied: removed a test boundary-escaping import, dropped a non-canonical schema key, added the type guards). I understand the code and ran the proof above myself. Prompts/session log available on request.

Closes #91011

## Notes
Net: +69 / ŌłÆ0 across 3 files (1 schema, 2 tests).
target.author: harjothkhara
